title: Swans 'taunted' and 'hit with sticks' at park - Bolton councillor

input.lead: Swans are being "taunted", "hit with sticks" and left "disgusting foods", a Greater Manchester councillor has said Sue Haworth, Reform UK councillor for Farnworth, in Bolton, told a full council meeting that children had reportedly been "taunting or hitting a swan with sticks" wi
input.summary: Swans are being "taunted", "hit with sticks" and left "disgusting foods", a Greater Manchester councillor has said Sue Haworth, Reform UK councillor for Farnworth, in Bolton, told a full council meeting that children had reportedly been "taunting or hitting a swan with sticks" wi
input.evidence_text: Swans are being "taunted", "hit with sticks" and left "disgusting foods", a Greater Manchester councillor has said Sue Haworth, Reform UK councillor for Farnworth, in Bolton, told a full council meeting that children had reportedly been "taunting or hitting a swan with sticks" with one swan found dead, according to volunteers at Moses Gate Country Park Haworth also said around 25 swan eggs had been reported missing at the park this year, according to the Local Democracy Reporting Service "One report said school age children were taunting or hitting a swan with sticks," Haworth said. "Disgusting food keeps being left for swans like doner kebab, spicy wedges, chicken with bones and sauce." "Such food is also thrown into the water," Haworth continued. "This food can kill the wildlife." The incidents were discussed as Bolton councillors debated a motion proposed by councillor Debbie Newall seeking to commit the authority to adopting an animal welfare charter, setting out its approach to protecting animals

title: New high-speed rail link and station set for Warrington

input.lead: A high-speed railway line and station are set to be built in Cheshire The new infrastructure will be built in Warrington, after plans were unveiled by leaders from Cheshire and Warrington Combined Authority (CWCA), on Friday The proposal would see the station built at Warrington
input.summary: A high-speed railway line and station are set to be built in Cheshire The new infrastructure will be built in Warrington, after plans were unveiled by leaders from Cheshire and Warrington Combined Authority (CWCA), on Friday The proposal would see the station built at Warrington
input.evidence_text: A high-speed railway line and station are set to be built in Cheshire The new infrastructure will be built in Warrington, after plans were unveiled by leaders from Cheshire and Warrington Combined Authority (CWCA), on Friday The proposal would see the station built at Warrington Bank Quay, according to the authority, which said it would be working with the Liverpool-Manchester Rail Board to bring the rail link to the region, according to the Local Democracy Reporting Service It comes after the region was given the government green light to form the CWCA in February, giving it control over transport-related powers. Residents are set to elect a mayor next year The link will come as part of phase two of the Northern Powerhouse Rail (NPR) project, a scheme aiming to deliver faster and more frequent trains across the North, through new and improved lines and stations The authority unveiled plans at a conference at Alderley Park on Friday - just days after the CWCA unveiled its £10m investment programme, as part of the 30-year mayoral investment fund settlement

title: Firefighters to travel to New York for 9/11 memorial stair climb

input.lead: A group of firefighters from the North West are preparing to travel to the United States later this year to take part in a memorial stair climb for the 25th anniversary of the 11 September 2001 terrorist attacks Firefighters from Lancashire Fire and Rescue Service and Greater Man
input.summary: A group of firefighters from the North West are preparing to travel to the United States later this year to take part in a memorial stair climb for the 25th anniversary of the 11 September 2001 terrorist attacks Firefighters from Lancashire Fire and Rescue Service and Greater Man
input.evidence_text: A group of firefighters from the North West are preparing to travel to the United States later this year to take part in a memorial stair climb for the 25th anniversary of the 11 September 2001 terrorist attacks Firefighters from Lancashire Fire and Rescue Service and Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) will join colleagues from across the UK and Ireland for the event in Atlanta, Georgia, on 5 September They will climb the equivalent of 110 floors, matching the height of the World Trade Center towers in New York, which were destroyed in the attacks killing thousands of people Most of the 343 firefighters who died were killed when the towers collapsed The climb will take place at the Atlanta Braves stadium, where firefighters from the United States, including some whose families were directly affected by the attacks, are also expected to take part The group are raising money for different charities

title: Conservatives name Greater Manchester mayoral by-election candidate

input.evidence_text: The Conservative Party has announced its candidate for the forthcoming Greater Manchester mayoral by-election Phil Eckersley currently represents Bowdon ward on Trafford Council where he is his party's deputy leader and shadow executive member for health and care The by-election on 30 July was called after Andy Burnham stood down earlier this month following his election as the new MP for Makerfield A full list of the announced candidates standing to be mayor can be found here Eckersley is a local businessman who founded Bridgewater Home Care in 2010 in Wigan and has "grown it into a successful care business supporting vulnerable and older people across the region, while employing more than 100 staff," the party said He previously served as president of the Greater Manchester Chamber of Commerce Wigan division

title: How Andy Burnham's school teacher inspired him to believe in himself

input.lead: "He needed a lot of persuading to apply because he felt that as a working-class boy, going off to Cambridge wasn't for him.
input.summary: "He needed a lot of persuading to apply because he felt that as a working-class boy, going off to Cambridge wasn't for him. He didn't believe in himself, but he did it, and the rest is history." These are the words of former English teacher Stephen Harrington on the advice he gav
input.evidence_text: "He needed a lot of persuading to apply because he felt that as a working-class boy, going off to Cambridge wasn't for him. He didn't believe in himself, but he did it, and the rest is history." These are the words of former English teacher Stephen Harrington on the advice he gave to a 16-year-old Andy Burnham in 1986 at St Aelred's Catholic High School in Newton-Le-Willows, Merseyside The man, now widely tipped to win any Labour leadership contest and become prime minister, has credited his former teacher with boosting his confidence at that pivotal time in his life "My son has been saying to me 'you've changed history Dad', but he likes a good phrase," Harrington said Burnham returned to Westminster last week as MP for Makerfield, after a by-election was triggered by the resignation of the constituency's Labour MP Josh Simons Following Sir Keir Starmer's announcement on Monday that he would step down, Burnham is currently the only candidate running in a leadership election in the Labour Party

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

final_reason: Intra-batch topic duplicate — same story kept from stronger source.

title: Greater Manchester's mayoral election explained

input.lead: Voters in all 10 boroughs will elect a new mayor to replace Andy Burnham on 30 July
input.summary: Voters in all 10 boroughs will elect a new mayor to replace Andy Burnham on 30 July
input.evidence_text: People living in Greater Manchester will head to the polls in July to vote for who they think should be the region's mayor Residents living in all 10 boroughs will vote in the Greater Manchester mayoral by-election on 30 July We take a look at why it is happening, what the timetable is, and how the supplementary vote system works The election was triggered when Andy Burnham, who held the office for nine years, was elected the MP for Makerfield in last Thursday's parliamentary by-election By law, an MP cannot sit in the House of Commons and hold a metropolitan mayor role that has powers over local policing The Greater Manchester mayoral contest has to take place within 35 days of the post becoming vacant

title: How do I register to vote in the May 2026 elections?

input.lead: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.summary: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.evidence_text: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May. Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

input.evidence_text: Football legend George Best's European Cup winner's medal has sold for more than £300,000 at auction Best scored in the 1968 final as Manchester United beat Benfica 4-1 after extra-time at Wembley The Northern Ireland international helped United to become the first English side to win the old European Cup, now known as the Champions League Best's medal was sold for £305,200 by auctioneers Bonhams in London after it had been offered by a private collector Best scored United's second goal in the final after he dribbled round the Benfica goalkeeper The Red Devils' victory came 10 years after the Munich Air Disaster in which eight members of Matt Busby's team, known as the Busby Babes, were among the 23 killed

title: Salford Cathedral to reopen after six-year £20m restoration

input.lead: A city's cathedral is set to reopen after a £20m restoration project which has taken six years Salford Cathedral, officially known as St John the Evangelist Cathedral, will be reopened to the public on 4 July The project included repairs to stonework, roofs and stained-glass wind
input.summary: A city's cathedral is set to reopen after a £20m restoration project which has taken six years Salford Cathedral, officially known as St John the Evangelist Cathedral, will be reopened to the public on 4 July The project included repairs to stonework, roofs and stained-glass wind
input.evidence_text: A city's cathedral is set to reopen after a £20m restoration project which has taken six years Salford Cathedral, officially known as St John the Evangelist Cathedral, will be reopened to the public on 4 July The project included repairs to stonework, roofs and stained-glass windows and implemented measures aimed at making the building environmentally sustainable "The parish are going to be absolutely blown away," Pauline Morgan chief operating officer for the diocese, told BBC Radio Manchester . "It is going to be an amazing place for them to come back and worship in." Morgan said the first services back in the cathedral were expected to be emotional "The Sunday Mass, when the bishop celebrates and the choir are back in here, you'll have everything coming together. The music, the congregation and the worship in such beautiful surroundings," she said

title: Manchester Royal Infirmary cinema shows Hollywood films to patients

input.lead: A cinema has opened at Manchester Royal Infirmary, which will be able to show blockbuster Hollywood films - sometimes even before their general UK release The movie theatre has 50 brightly coloured seats with extra legroom - as well as space for wheelchairs, hospital beds and ess
input.summary: A cinema has opened at Manchester Royal Infirmary, which will be able to show blockbuster Hollywood films - sometimes even before their general UK release The movie theatre has 50 brightly coloured seats with extra legroom - as well as space for wheelchairs, hospital beds and ess
input.evidence_text: A cinema has opened at Manchester Royal Infirmary, which will be able to show blockbuster Hollywood films - sometimes even before their general UK release The movie theatre has 50 brightly coloured seats with extra legroom - as well as space for wheelchairs, hospital beds and essential medical equipment Elliot Martin from Bury, who saw the cinema's first screening of the latest Minions movie, said: "It is brilliant for patients like me and I hope I can come back again." The 13 year-old, a patient at the Royal Manchester Children's Hospital, added: "I feel absolutely brilliant and I'd love to watch a Star Wars film here." The MediCinema is based in a former lecture theatre, close to the main entrance of the MRI, and it will be available to patients across the Manchester University NHS Foundation Trust's hospitals The facility launched with a special screening of the children's feature film Minions and Monsters, ahead of its official release in UK cinemas on 1 July

The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

input.evidence_text: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May. Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

The proportion of older cases in the backlog rose by 24 per cent in the previous year (17,819) and 6 percent compared wi However, the figures show that nearly half of crown courts reduced the size of their backlog of cases during the first three months of the year

It was a different place altogether,” she recalls. "It was hard, they didn’t tell us that in England you had to have fire in your house, it was freezing, always freezing “All we had on was cotton jerseys and warm clothes, nothing else.” A member of the Windrush generation, Locita was among the thousands of eager men and women who were invited to rebuild the United Kingdom after it was decimated by WWII

And in Salford , a second teenage boy was reported as missing with a huge water search still underway at the popular Clifton Count The tragedies bring the number of open water deaths reported in the UK during the recent heatwave to seven. Police and fire service bosses have again stressed the dangers of swimming in, or entering, open water - warning strong currents, low water temperatures and debris beneath Greater Manchester Police Chief Inspector Helen Baxter said: "We remind the public to please avoid being tempted to cool off in reservoirs, rivers, canals or ponds. We all want to enjoy the warm weather , but please make sure you do so in a safe way." The force said emergency services were called to reports of a boy getting into difficulty in the water at Cowbury Reservoir at around 6.30pm on Saturday night

But the proposals have b News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 16:41, 28 Jun 2026 For Justin Kennedy, the landmark news serves as a chance for justice to finally be served. As a young man, he endured unimaginable pain in the name of religion, forced to 'pray the gay away' and undergo rit In what is now recognised as conversion therapy, Justin, now a minister of the same Baptist church that encouraged abuse he faced in his late teen years and early 20s, says over half of his life was 'robbed' to cruel practises and ceremonies His comments come after the Government made a huge announcement on Thursday (June 25), finally outlining the draft of the Conversion Practises Bill. The landmark law will seek to protect members of the LGBTQ+ community and criminalise attempts to change or suppress someone's sexu It could create new criminal offences for carrying out conversion practices that cause serious harm, alarm or distress. The legislation would also make it illegal to encourage or assist such practices outside England or Wales This is because laws have so far failed to truly tackle the 'unique nature' of conversion practices, with victims reporting abuse ranging from threats and manipulation to physical and sexual violence

Police and fire service bosses have again stressed the dangers of swimming in, or entering, open water - warning strong currents, low water temperatures and debris beneath Greater Manchester Police Chief Inspector Helen Baxter said: "We remind the public to please avoid being tempted to cool off in reservoirs, rivers, canals or ponds. title: 'Those years destroyed my spirit, soul and body, now I couldn't feel any happier'

input.lead: LGBTQ+ rights 'Those years destroyed my spirit, soul and body, now I couldn't feel any happier' After years of abuse, members of the LGBTQ+ community may now finally be protected by a landmark law banning conversion practises.
input.summary: LGBTQ+ rights 'Those years destroyed my spirit, soul and body, now I couldn't feel any happier' After years of abuse, members of the LGBTQ+ community may now finally be protected by a landmark law banning conversion practises. But the proposals have b News James Holt Senior Live
input.evidence_text: LGBTQ+ rights 'Those years destroyed my spirit, soul and body, now I couldn't feel any happier' After years of abuse, members of the LGBTQ+ community may now finally be protected by a landmark law banning conversion practises. But the proposals have b News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 16:41, 28 Jun 2026 For Justin Kennedy, the landmark news serves as a chance for justice to finally be served. As a young man, he endured unimaginable pain in the name of religion, forced to 'pray the gay away' and undergo rit In what is now recognised as conversion therapy, Justin, now a minister of the same Baptist church that encouraged abuse he faced in his late teen years and early 20s, says over half of his life was 'robbed' to cruel practises and ceremonies His comments come after the Government made a huge announcement on Thursday (June 25), finally outlining the draft of the Conversion Practises Bill. The landmark law will seek to protect members of the LGBTQ+ community and criminalise attempts to change or suppress someone's sexu It could create new criminal offences for carrying out conversion practices that cause serious harm, alarm or distress. The legislation would also make it illegal to encourage or assist such practices outside England or Wales This is because laws have so far failed to truly tackle the 'unique nature' of conversion practices, with victims reporting abuse ranging from threats and manipulation to physical and sexual violence

input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester mayoral election 2026 Conservative Party selects Trafford councillor and businessman as Greater Manchester Mayor candidate The Conservates have announced Phil Eckersley as their candidate for Greater Manchester Mayor Comments News I The Conservative Party has announced a Trafford councillor and businessman as its candidate to be the next mayor of Greater Manchester. Phil Eckersley represents the Bowdon ward, where he is his party's deputy leader and shadow executive member for health and care Coun Eckersley founded Bridgewater Care Home in Wigan in 2010, which employs more than 100 people. He previously served as president of the Greater Manchester Chamber of Commerce Wigan division He said: “Greater Manchester is my home, and it’s a region with enormous potential, but too often decisions are made with a focus on Manchester city centre – with the outer boroughs getting left behind “As a business owner, father and local councillor, I understand the challenges facing families, businesses and communities across our city-region “From pressure on public services and concerns about crime and antisocial behaviour, to congestion, housing and the need for stronger local economies, these are issues I have dealt with directly throughout my professional and public life

title: The court backlog that could take 300 years to clear

input.lead: Courts The court backlog that could take 300 years to clear Sexual offences make up a growing proportion of cases with the longest delays in crown courts in England and Wales, according to analysis of the figures.
input.summary: Courts The court backlog that could take 300 years to clear Sexual offences make up a growing proportion of cases with the longest delays in crown courts in England and Wales, according to analysis of the figures. Paul Britton reports
input.evidence_text: Courts The court backlog that could take 300 years to clear Sexual offences make up a growing proportion of cases with the longest delays in crown courts in England and Wales, according to analysis of the figures. Paul Britton reports. View 3 Images M News Paul Britton Reporter 06:04, 29 Jun 2026 The backlog in cases at Manchester's two main crown courts has fallen, new figures reveal amid continued calls for widespread change to the judicial system and major reform to cut jury trials Official Ministry of Justice figures showed record numbers of victims are waiting more than a year for their cases to be heard by crown courts in England and Wales, with nearly a quarter of these being for sex crimes Overall, there were 80,061 outstanding cases in the crown court backlog at the end of March, the latest figures available Of these, the number which have been waiting to be dealt with for a year or more has hit a record high of 22,124, accounting for 29 per cent of all open cases. The proportion of older cases in the backlog rose by 24 per cent in the previous year (17,819) and 6 percent compared wi However, the figures show that nearly half of crown courts reduced the size of their backlog of cases during the first three months of the year

title: The remarkable journey of Aunty Locita

input.lead: The remarkable journey of Aunty Locita She arrived in Manchester expecting opportunity but found prejudice instead.
input.summary: The remarkable journey of Aunty Locita She arrived in Manchester expecting opportunity but found prejudice instead. Rather than accept it, Locita Brandy spent the next seven decades fighting for children, championing her community and helping shape th News Ramazani Mwamba News re
input.evidence_text: The remarkable journey of Aunty Locita She arrived in Manchester expecting opportunity but found prejudice instead. Rather than accept it, Locita Brandy spent the next seven decades fighting for children, championing her community and helping shape th News Ramazani Mwamba News reporter 19:36, 28 Jun 2026 The thing that Locita Brandy remembers most about coming to the UK is how cold it was The 91-year-old was in her early twenties when she traded tropical Nevis for the poorly insulated redbrick terraced houses and unpredictable weather of south Manchester in 1956 “It was harrowing, it was cold. It was a different place altogether,” she recalls. "It was hard, they didn’t tell us that in England you had to have fire in your house, it was freezing, always freezing “All we had on was cotton jerseys and warm clothes, nothing else.” A member of the Windrush generation, Locita was among the thousands of eager men and women who were invited to rebuild the United Kingdom after it was decimated by WWII

input.evidence_text: LGBTQ+ rights 'Those years destroyed my spirit, soul and body, now I couldn't feel any happier' After years of abuse, members of the LGBTQ+ community may now finally be protected by a landmark law banning conversion practises. But the proposals have b News James Holt Senior Live and Breaking News Reporter 16:41, 28 Jun 2026 For Justin Kennedy, the landmark news serves as a chance for justice to finally be served. As a young man, he endured unimaginable pain in the name of religion, forced to 'pray the gay away' and undergo rit In what is now recognised as conversion therapy, Justin, now a minister of the same Baptist church that encouraged abuse he faced in his late teen years and early 20s, says over half of his life was 'robbed' to cruel practises and ceremonies His comments come after the Government made a huge announcement on Thursday (June 25), finally outlining the draft of the Conversion Practises Bill. The landmark law will seek to protect members of the LGBTQ+ community and criminalise attempts to change or suppress someone's sexu It could create new criminal offences for carrying out conversion practices that cause serious harm, alarm or distress. The legislation would also make it illegal to encourage or assist such practices outside England or Wales This is because laws have so far failed to truly tackle the 'unique nature' of conversion practices, with victims reporting abuse ranging from threats and manipulation to physical and sexual violence

The court heard how Bevan was stabbed 25 times in his cell with an improvised weapon on November 4 last year His body was then placed in his bed and covered with a blanket to give the impression he was asleep. CCTV footage from inside the prison showed the three killers following Bevan into his cell. They then left less than five minutes later and Bevan was never seen alive again Fellows was also seen checking on the body to ensure he had not been discovered before prison officers found Bevan dead inside his cell the following morning

input.lead: Trains Probe launched into how two Northern trains came within 12 seconds of catastrophe The 'dangerous occurence' involved the 9.57pm Manchester Victoria to Leeds train News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 15:51, 27 Jun 2026 View Image A Northern train at V Two Northern trains were inv
input.summary: Trains Probe launched into how two Northern trains came within 12 seconds of catastrophe The 'dangerous occurence' involved the 9.57pm Manchester Victoria to Leeds train News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 15:51, 27 Jun 2026 View Image A Northern train at V Two Northern trains were inv
input.evidence_text: Trains Probe launched into how two Northern trains came within 12 seconds of catastrophe The 'dangerous occurence' involved the 9.57pm Manchester Victoria to Leeds train News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 15:51, 27 Jun 2026 View Image A Northern train at V Two Northern trains were involved in a 'dangerous' 45mph near miss An investigation has been launched following the incident involving the 9.57pm Manchester Victoria to Leeds service and another out-of-service Northern train on May 7. It came after the driver of the out-of-service train was given permission by the signaller to move closer to a s But, unbeknown to the driver, the train's cab was already past the signal. That resulted in the train making what the Rail Accident Investigation Branch described as an 'uncontrolled movement' towards Todmorden Viaduct Junction The driver realised what had happened and brought the train to stop close to the junction. Just 12 seconds later, at 10.28pm, the Leeds-bound train came through the junction at 45mph The RAIB say no injuries or damage were caused as a result of the near miss. They say it came during a 'period of revised working arrangements that had temporarily increased the number of shunting movements involving Todmorden West Curve'

Paul Britton Reporter . . 11:54, 27 Jun 2026 . . . . The blaze in Cheetham Hill (Image: David Murphy) . . Everything we know about huge fire in Cheetham Hill . Dozens of firefighters were scrambled to a major blaze in Cheetham Hill - with roads remaining closed on Saturday morning. Residents living in the surrounding area were told to keep windows and doors shut due to smoke.. A clean-up operation was launched at the scene near Manchester Fort on Saturday morning, with fire crews still at the scene. A number of roads were sealed off.. Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service confirmed 10 fire engines attended the scene on Broughton Street overnight. The blaze, according to the fire brigade, involved 'several' single-storey commercial buildings.. A video taken at the scene at the height of the blaze shows huge plumes of smoke and a raging fire.. In a statement, the fire service said: "Firefighters arrived quickly and used two hose reel jets, one hand-held jet and an aerial appliance to bring the fire under control. Nearby residents are advised to keep their windows and doors closed if there is visible smoke in their area.". The fire service also warned of the possibility of 'low-lying smoke passing north in the Cheetham Hill area'. Road closures, meanwhile, remain in place for Broughton Street and Bent Street.. title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election: What happens next with Greater Manchester mayoralty?

input.lead: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade.
input.summary: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A
input.evidence_text: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

Everyone in court read the Facebook post in silence. “This is just the starter though,” Westgaph had written on top of an image of himself staring at the camera with his shirt unbutton This was the denouement of a tough hour-long cross-examination of Westgaph, in which it often looked like he was struggling to explain why he had initiated this lawsuit in the first place. Now she has her eyes on a bigger prize Before politics, one interest dominated the life of Geraldine Coggins, leader of the Trafford Greens, candidate for Greater Manchester mayor, and that was metaphysical nihilism. The now-politician harbours a lifelong love of philosophy, studying the subject up and down the UK and What is metaphysical nihilism? input.evidence_text: Aged ten and eight, my brother and I appeared on the Saturday morning horror-comedy show Ross Lee’s Ghoulies . Our task was this: we were to dress as zombies and crouch in the bushes at the local golf course while our Dad enjoyed a morning of hooks and slices. When he reached the There followed the ghoulies, four of them, a Nickelodeon-troupe of pasty white zombies waddling out from the shrubbery, followed by serial prankster and TV host Ross Lee (who would eventually hit dizzying heights with the Keith Lemon Sketch Show). When we got to the studio in Lon Chezdan Mills had his own post-TV fame realisation that same year, 2009. His came at a bus stop The show Chezdan had been on had a viewership more than a touch higher than Ross Lee’s Ghoulies . At least, while I don’t think Chezdan can recall seeing me and my brother standing proud before the Scareometer, I distinctly recall watching him, arguing with an irate Pastor on BBC At that time, British reality TV operated under a defining principle. The key was to be as nasty as possible to ordinary people, and to make them appear as ludicrous as possible for the entertainment of the viewing public. Do I really want the top job?’ And Kevin must be saying ‘Of course you do An According to another former colleague of both men, Lee is a brilliant operator in the shadows: the “omnipresent” man who executes the mayor’s will and smooths his path. “I’ve never known him to be unkind or unfair to anyone,” this person says, pushing back against Lee’s critics Polling day in Makerfield is now just a week away, and our intrepid reporter Lucy McLaughlin is in the constituency right now, sniffing out stories. But in today’s edition we thought we would try to answer a question lots of people have been asking us recently: who are the people Lee is the first name on the list, so we’ve been finding out how an Essex boy who took an unusual path into politics has become so indispensable to the mayor

title: The Council wants to bring hundreds of empty homes back into use - this is how you can spot one and report it

input.lead: Since the beginning of the year, a major review has been underway around the city’s draft Strategy for bringing empty homes back into use to help meet demand for affordable homes across the city – while also improving communities by tackling
input.summary: Since the beginning of the year, a major review has been underway around the city’s draft Strategy for bringing empty homes back into use to help meet demand for affordable homes across the city – while also improving communities by tackling
input.evidence_text: Since the beginning of the year, a major review has been underway around the city’s draft Strategy for bringing empty homes back into use to help meet demand for affordable homes across the city – while also improving communities by tackling empty homes that can blight an area Following a major public consultation, the Council is now acting on feedback to accelerate plans to bring as many long-term empty homes back into active use as possible – proposals that saw strong public backing from residents (83% of the respondents to the recent consultation ag Although the number of long-term empty homes in Manchester remains close to an historic low – as the Council has managed to reduce the number more than half since 2013 – every void property is a missed opportunity to provide a family a place to live, reduce homelessness and lesse Feedback from Manchester people has confirmed the impact in the city’s neighbourhoods. 86% of respondents to a recent consultation expressed concern about empty homes (51% very concerned) with the majority also reporting that they had seen empty homes fall into disrepair, become What to do if there is an empty home in your community An empty home is often easily recognisable on a street. The easiest way to report an empty home is to report the issue online or by emailing emptyhomesteam@manchester.gov.uk

title: Repurposed flats will help homeless Manchester families

input.lead: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.summary: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.evidence_text: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families The property in Greenwood Road, Woodhouse Park, has been acquired by the Council from registered housing provider Mosscare St Vincent’s as part of wider plans to boost the city’s stock of publicly-owned, quality temporary accommodation Work is due to start this summer, and be completed in December this year, to refurbish the property as 17 self-contained flats for homeless families from Wythenshawe and south Manchester All flats will be refurbished, including new kitchens and bathrooms in each case. Two new laundry rooms for shared use will also be created, and gardens to the rear will have raised bedding for residents’ use The new accommodation will see families supported by an accommodation team based on site to help them move on as quickly as possible into permanent settled tenancies The Greenwood Road initiative is the latest example of the Council’s drive to increase its temporary accommodation stock across the city to reduce the number of out-of-area placements, helping people who become homeless to stay closer to their previous homes and support networks

title: The Greater Manchester Neurodevelopmental (ND) Early Help Model - Stockport Council

input.lead: You may have heard that Greater Manchester is introducing a new way of deciding which children and young people receive an NHS autism or ADHD assessment, and how quickly they are seen.
input.summary: You may have heard that Greater Manchester is introducing a new way of deciding which children and young people receive an NHS autism or ADHD assessment, and how quickly they are seen. We know this may raise questions or concerns for families In this article, we’re sharing clear
input.evidence_text: You may have heard that Greater Manchester is introducing a new way of deciding which children and young people receive an NHS autism or ADHD assessment, and how quickly they are seen. We know this may raise questions or concerns for families In this article, we’re sharing clear and simple information about what these changes mean in Stockport, and where you can get advice and support. Some local details are still being finalised, and we’ll add updates as soon as they are confirmed. For more information, you can also We understand how hard it can be waiting for an assessment, and how difficult it can be to know where to turn for help in the meantime. That’s why we’ve introduced a new way of getting early support from the moment you first notice signs that your child or young person may be aut We know this is a lot to take in. If you’d like to find out more, ask questions, or talk things through, you’re very welcome to come along to one of our information drop-in sessions The Strategic Lead for Neurodivergence in Stockport will be attending the following drop-in sessions supported by the Child and Adolescent Mental Health Service (CAMHS), Parents and Carers Together Stockport (PACTStockport), and Stockport Parents of ADHD Children in Education (SP In-person session with CAMHS and PACTStockport on Monday 11th May 10.30am-12pm, at Alma Lodge, 149 Buxton Road, Stockport, SK2 6EL. Refreshments will be provided. Please reserve a spot for this session

In total, h Investigations by our counter fraud team and Greater Manchester Police, which were supported by intelligence from the National Fraud Initiative, revealed that he had deliberately falsified his business lease documents and that he had only ever used a pigeonhole at the industrial Mr Saddique admitted 2 counts of fraud by false representation at Manchester’s Minshull Street Crown Court on Friday, 5 June 2026 In addition, he also pleaded guilty to theft of a motor vehicle at the same hearing, after he ordered a car costing more than £58,000 from an Audi dealership and cancelled the payment while the car was already on its way to him. title: Bolton Library marks centenary of the 1926 General Strike - Bolton Council

input.lead: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display
input.summary: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display
input.evidence_text: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display 100 years ago, in May 1926, millions of workers across Britain took part in the General Strike The strike originated as a dispute between coal mine owners and miners over reduced wages and longer working hours When the Trades Union Congress (TUC) declared their support for the miners, other industrial workers - including those in transport, engineering, and textiles - also walked out, and it became the first ever General Strike in British history The strike was called on 3 May 1926 and lasted nine days, where the first few days of the strike saw the transport network in Bolton grind to a halt without bus and train drivers Food deliveries were delayed, and some textile mills stopped operating

These have included Holocaust survivors, ‘witches’ and Victorian factory workers. She likes to link these stories to her own experiences, such as fostering refugees and Accessibility is at the heart of everything Sally creates. Such as writing audio descriptions in the form of poetry, writing easy reads, and delivering neurodivergent friendly tours You can find Sally most Thursdays in our Everyday Art School space on our Mezzanine

title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

An untested crew. A long shot no one expects to work Somewhere far beyond Earth, a barely-functional spacecraft drifts on a mission most people have already written off. The real scientists are doing the real work back home. Sent into the void: whoever’s left Clarke wants to get back. Baby won’t leave the ship. Witney is staying away from their mother. They were never meant to be astronauts. input.evidence_text: Manchester Rum Festival 2026 - Manchester’s Finest Manchester Rum Festival 2026 Sat 4th Jul, 2026 - New Century Manchester Rum Festival 2026 New Century, 34 Hanover St, Manchester M4 4AH Sat 4th Jul, 2026 £25 Get tickets Directions events Go to Manchester's Finest Facebook page Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Manchester Rum Festival returns for its 10th anniversary this summer with a huge celebration of global rum culture, bringing together producers from Jamaica, Guyana, Martinique, Saint Lucia, Bermuda, Japan, Belize, Mauritius and beyond. There’ll be debut pours from brands including Crossfire Hurricane, the Jamaican rum co-founded by the Rolling Stones, alongside Windrush 1027, inspired by the passengers who arrived in Britain aboard HMT Empire Windrush in 1948. Expect cocktail collaborations from MONIN and Manchester venues, Rum Ramble seminars with international speakers, exclusive anniversary bottlings available only to attendees, and a full-day soundtrack of Soca and Reggae from festival favourite DJ Dom. title: NATHANIEL RATELIFF & THE NIGHT SWEATS

input.lead: Nathaniel Rateliff is a striking, emotive tenor vocalist and songwriter whose work runs the gamut from simple folk and Americana to roots rock & roll to vintage rhythm & blues.
input.summary: Nathaniel Rateliff is a striking, emotive tenor vocalist and songwriter whose work runs the gamut from simple folk and Americana to roots rock & roll to vintage rhythm & blues. He and his band the Night Sweats are based in Denver, Colorado
input.evidence_text: Nathaniel Rateliff is a striking, emotive tenor vocalist and songwriter whose work runs the gamut from simple folk and Americana to roots rock & roll to vintage rhythm & blues. He and his band the Night Sweats are based in Denver, Colorado. After fronting two earlier bands, Ratel Rateliff had begun writing songs that required a band, so he formed the Night Sweats. Their rowdy retro sound was deeply influenced by vintage soul, R&B and gospel traditions, Van Morrison and the Band. The move marks a new chapter for the pair, who will also launch Boutikk, a neighbouring provisions store inspired by Scandinavian food culture and tr Originally opened in 2020, OSMA quickly established itself as one of Greater Manchester’s most acclaimed neighbourhood restaurants. Named after a combination of Oslo and Manchester, the restaurant blended Stoermann-Naess’ Norwegian heritage with Heron’s classical culinary trainin The return will see Heron resume her role as Executive Chef, with a menu centred around seasonal produce, fresh seafood, local meats and vegetable-led dishes. A number of OSMA’s most popular plates are expected to make a comeback, alongside the restaurant’s well-known sharing Sun While the restaurant occupies one side of the development, Boutikk will introduce a new offering to the city centre. The move follows a significant decline in placements, with FE Wee To me, those two things are closely connected, for years, the conversation around skills and early careers has focused heavily on supply. More qualifications, more bootcamps, more people trained, more pathways into industry Those things matter. But creating talent is only half the equation. The other half is making sure employers have the capacity, confidence and support to absorb that talent Over the last year, Manchester Digital has been running TConnect, a pilot supported by the Gatsby Foundation and Greater Manchester Combined Authority that aimed to make it easier for employers to engage with T Level placements. Like most pilots, it raised as many questions as it One thing became clear very quickly. Hosted on infrastructure operated by Nublue, part of the CTI Group, the platform represents the next stage in CTI Digital’s wider strategy to support organisations navigating the rapidly evolving AI landscape. Chris Burgess, group CEO of CTI Digital, said: “Over the last two years, AI adoption has accelerated at an incredible pace. Manchester’s CTI Digital appoints serial entrepreneur as new CEO “Tools such as ChatGPT, Claude and Copilot have demonstrated enormous value, but they have also created new challenges around ownership, governance, cost and control. “Many organisations have understandably focused on what AI can do. The Medieval Quarter is the perfect place to soak in all that history. It is here where you will see the oldest public library in the English-speaking world, Chetham’s Library . For the four women at its centre, music is something embodied and grounding – felt in breath, pulse and the vibrations of our vocal cords. Working f For decades, Meredith Monk has been one of the great escape artists of contemporary music, making work that spills into choreography, theatre, film and installation. Treating the voice as an instrument, her music often feels somehow beyond language, less interested in what sound Tellingly, her 2005 string quartet is called Stringsongs, and was conceived as an act of vocal translation. “I explored using instruments to create unexpected textures and sounds in much the same way that I have worked with the voice over many years”, Monk writes. The quartet’s f Monk’s rarely performed Backlight pushes that idea further, into a larger ensemble. Here she plays with shadow and light through flickering layers of dissonance and texture. From its roots in manufacturing and industry to today’s studios filled with artists, designers and creative technologists, making is part of its DNA. City of Making at the New Adelphi Exhibition Gallery captures that spirit, tracing a lineage Running as part of Salford 100, the exhibition brings together works from the University of Salford Art Collection, new pieces by staff from the School of Art, Media and Creative Technology, and material drawn from the University Archives. In doing so, it creates a conversation a Prints, sculpture, drawing and mixed media works explore making as both process and philosophy. Some pieces foreground experimentation – form, material, function pushed and pulled into new shapes. Others turn to recycled materials and environmental questions, weighing production The exhibition also reflects Salford’s collaborative spirit. Partnerships with Hot Bed Press, Islington Mill, Paradise Works and Salford Museum & Art Gallery speak to a shared commitment to sustaining creative practice locally and regionally. Altrincham’s charter dates back to 1290, it’s location at the intersection of the Cheshire farm belt and the commercial commuter zone of Manchester means the town has a long and important history. input.evidence_text: Bolton Car Boot Sale | Bolton Sunday Car Boot Sale | Times, Prices & Info Skip to content 210+ UK car boot sales — find one near you this weekend Type your postcode or town to find sales near you Search car boot sales About This Car Boot Sale Bolton Car Boot Sale is one of the North West's biggest weekly boot sales, held in the hardstanding car park of the Macron Stadium (formerly Reebok), Burnden Way, Horwich, Bolton BL6 6JW. It runs every Sunday from February right through to December, drawing over 300 sellers and thousands of bargain hunters each week. Sellers can arrive and set up from 6:00 AM, with buyers welcomed in from 7:00 AM. Entry costs just £1 per car for buyers. Pitches for sellers are £14 for a car, £20 for a van and £3 for a trailer, with regular and corner pitches available on request. Bring exact change to keep the queue moving, and call organiser Andrew on 07470784644 for more details. Expect hundreds of stalls across the concrete hardstanding pitches, so it goes ahead in most weather. On-site you'll find a 5-star catering service, doughnuts and coffee, ice cream and desserts, a meat auction, fresh fruit and veg, plants, and retro sweet stalls. The sale is dog-friendly, has male and female toilets, ample parking, and kids' rides and bouncy castles to keep little ones entertained. Last confirmed open: 8 June 2026. title: City in Asia: Our previous Atleti clashes

input.lead: City in Asia: Our previous Atleti clashes
input.summary: City and Atleti had never met competitively until the 2021/22 season. The two sides had avoided each other until being paired for a Champions League quarter-final over two legs It was 5 April when Diego Simeone brought his disciplined outfit the Etihad, looking to take a positive
input.evidence_text: City and Atleti had never met competitively until the 2021/22 season. The two sides had avoided each other until being paired for a Champions League quarter-final over two legs It was 5 April when Diego Simeone brought his disciplined outfit the Etihad, looking to take a positive result back to the Spanish capital In a war of attrition, the Blues largely dominated but it wasn’t until the 70th-minute that Kevin De Bruyne broke the deadlock, latching on to Phil Foden’s delightful threaded pass before placing a low angled shot past the keeper with what would be the only goal of the game Atleti felt they were very much in the tie and confident they could overturn a 1-0 deficit at the Estadio Metropolitano, but they would take on a very different Manchester City – one prepared to dig in and scrap in what was an intense physical battle There had been five bookings in the first leg shared between the teams – there was eight in the second leg, including a red card for Felipe in an ill-tempered end to the game But City stood firm, held Atleti 0-0 and won a semi-final berth with Atleti’s bitter rivals Real Madrid

input.evidence_text: 5 июля 2026 года в Big Belly Bar & Comedy Club в Лондоне состоится выступление комиков Андрея Бережко и Павла Пинчука Хотите стать автором или авторкой комедии? Тогда попробуйте создать её вместе с Пашей Пинчуком и Андреем Бережко Вас ждет стендап-шоу, главная изюминка которого – импровизация. Это как сидеть с друзьями в вечернем баре и рассказывать, что смешного произошло с вами, пока вы не виделись Паша Пинчук и Андрей Бережко хотят, чтобы вы хохотали искренне и непринужденно и одновременно поддерживали ВСУ. Для этого они презентуют свой фирменный стендап-импровизацию, где всё идет не по плану, как и в жизни Их уже ждут в Испании, Португалии, Польше и, конечно, в Великобритании, так что не пропустите это лондонское выступление Все средства, собранные во время тура, будут направлены на поддержку Вооруженных Сил Украины

title: Андрей Кравченко

input.lead: Андрей Кравченко at Brixton Jamm
input.summary: Андрей Кравченко в Лондоне! 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне
input.evidence_text: 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне Андрей Кравченко покорил сердца тысяч слушателей благодаря своему уникальному голосу, харизме и способности превращать личные истории в песни, близкие каждому. В его творчестве смешиваются романтическая лирика, современное эстрадное звучание и проникновенная искренность После серии аншлаговых выступлений на самых престижных сценах Украины, среди которых Жовтневый Дворец и Дворец “Украина”, певец продолжает свое концертное путешествие и приглашает поклонников на особую встречу в Лондоне Концерты Андрея Кравченко известны своей теплой атмосферой: здесь нет дистанции между сценой и залом, ведь каждое выступление превращается в откровенный разговор на языке музыки. Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! Подарите себе вечер настоящей романтики, красивой музыки и искренних чувств вместе с Андреем Кравченко

title: Bioengineering Pathways: Academia and Entrepreneurship
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

The session will highlight how scientists can navigate careers spanning academia, industry, and entrepreneurship. Drawing on the experiences of our speakers, we will explore complementary career trajectories, including:

  • ​Transitioning across international biotech ecosystems and startup environments

  • ​Combining academic leadership with company co-founding

  • ​Bridging fundamental research with commercial application, particularly in sustainability and climate biotech

​Whether you are exploring commercialisation, developing new technologies, or considering future career directions, join us to hear inspiring stories and learn how Nucleate UK partners with Advanced Research and Invention Agency (ARIA) to accelerate ambitious ideas.

Speakers:
Lucia Burdisso, PhD - Lucia is the Chief Scientific Officer at Deep Blue Biotech, where she leads the development of a cyanobacterial synthetic biology platform for sustainable, CO₂-based chemical production. A biotechnologist with a PhD and more than 10 years of experience in R&D across academia and biotech startups, Lucia has worked in 10 laboratories across 4 countries, including startups in Argentina and the San Francisco Bay Area, and is now based in Sheffield.

Ressa Lebedinec, PhD - Ressa is a molecular mycologist and Senior Biomolecular Engineer at The University of Manchester Biotechnology Institute, with over 10 years of experience in fungal biology, biomolecular engineering, and sustainable biotechnology. She is the co-founder of mushroom+, a deep-tech platform formed in 2025 within the Carbon13 venture builder ecosystem.
mushroom+ is developing technologies that enhance the protein content and performance of mushrooms, transforming existing mushroom farms into climate-positive protein hubs. By increasing protein yield and nutritional density per kilogram of biomass, the company aims to address critical environmental and economic challenges in food production, without requiring the food system to be rebuilt from scratch.

Daniele Parisi, PhD - Daniele is a Senior Scientist at C3, where he leads efforts in scaling up fermentation processes and solving challenges related to circular biomanufacturing. With over a decade of experience in sustainability-driven research and development, he brings deep expertise in fermentation, downstream processing (DSP), and biocatalysis. Daniele holds a PhD in Molecular Biology and Biochemistry, and has contributed to both early-stage sustainability-focused startups and global companies, including his recent work with Croda.

Drinks and mexican food will be provided during the networking session with peers and potential collaborators.

title: Are we there yet? A journey into transport data
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

Ever wondered what's powering your favourite journey planner

input.lead: Are we there yet? A journey into transport data

enrichment_health.status:
failed: URL can't contain control characters. "/

Ever wondered what's powering your favourite journey planner? Or how CityMapper somehow knows your bus is running three minutes late before the driver does?

Open data has quietly transformed the way we move around — from the apps we tap before leaving the house to the behind-the-scenes feeds that keep public transport ticking. It underpins a huge range of transport services we rely on every day, and with the Bus Services Act 2025 pushing more bus data into the open, there's never been a better moment to take stock of where we've got to — and where we still need to go.

We'll be looking at road transport too: open data on scheduled roadworks and live fuel prices has given drivers a fighting chance of making genuinely informed decisions about their journeys.

At this event we want to dig into the great things open transport data has made possible, hear some real examples of services and insights it's enabled, and have an honest conversation about the challenges that remain for people getting around.

" (found at least ' ')

title: CONEXEN Manchester July
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

Registration needed: https:/conexen.net/conexen-events/conexen-manchester-july/

Welcome to CONEXEN Manchester – A warm and welcoming community for professionals and founders in biopharma, life sciences, and health tech in Manchester! Here, we invite you to join us for an informal and friendly gathering where connections are made, knowledge is shared, and friendships are formed. Come with an open heart and a willingness to “listen” rather than “sell.”

​​We will be hosting our Manchester event at the amazing space of Cloud 23 Cocktail Bar. It is a casual setting where we can come together, exchange greetings, engage in conversation, and simply enjoy a pleasant time.

​​Picture this as more than just an event, it’s a life sciences community gathering. Our focus is on providing you with an opportunity to meet fellow professionals and founders in a relaxed and amiable environment. We’ll share conversations over delicious food and drinks, and leave with a sense of excitement and a pocketful of new contacts and ideas.

​​Join us for an evening surrounded by incredible people, and let’s make it a night to remember. Looking forward to sharing good company and creating memorable connections!

​Theme: The Forces Reshaping Work & Culture in Life Sciences.

Join a focused discussion led by Paul Bradley-Law on the forces redefining culture in a hybrid world. Our panellists will explore how hybrid working, evolving HR trends, leadership style, and a multi-generational workforce are reshaping how organisations operate, alongside the growing influence of AI in the workplace.

We’ll examine how trust is built through clarity and candour in uncertain times—without defaulting to micromanagement—and how to address “proximity bias” to ensure fair visibility, recognition, and opportunity across distributed teams.

Join peers shaping the North-West Life Sciences ecosystem and driving the region’s growing influence.

​Moderator: Paul Bradley-Law (Founder, The Great People Machine)

​Panelists:

  • Ike Cooke, Business Growth & Commercialisation Manager, LYVA Labs

  • Julia Buckler, Senior Director, Site Head, Qiagen

​The format of the night:

  • ​18:00 Doors open – drinks, networking and meeting great people.

  • ​18:30 Talks start

  • ​19:15 Drinks, food and networking.

  • ​20:30 End of the night – you leave with new contacts and fresh ideas.

​​Venue: Cloud 23 Cocktail Bar – The Manchester Deansgate Hotel, 303 Deansgate, Manchester M3 4LQ, United Kingdom.

This event is proudly supported by Nexa Life Sciences and Kadans Science Partner, hosted by Kadans Science Partner.

​

​And finally…

​This is a ticketed event. Food and drinks for the night are included.

​Please let your colleagues and industry friends know about us, we hope to build it into a great resource for the ecosystem over the coming months and years. Attendance will be taken at this event. If you register but don’t show up, this has a knock-on effect on event arrangements. If it happens 3 times, we may need to reconsider your participation. This is simply a measure to allow appropriate respect to the organisers, speakers, sponsors, and your fellow Conexers.

​See you there!

​Ashley, Julia, and Sajid
Manchester Organisers

​Tickets FAQs: https:/www.conexen.net/faq

Questions

title: Unlocking New Possibilities with the Oracle AI Ecosystem
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

An executive innovation experience, connecting AI, data and applications to real business outcomes.

Unlocking New Possibilities with the Oracle AI Ecosystem

Join us at Oracle's offices in Moorgate, London for Kovaion Connect 2026 - a focused executive briefing showing how Oracle Applications, Data, AI, OCI and Oracle APEX come together to help organisations innovate faster, operate smarter and unlock measurable business outcomes.

This is a half-day session built for senior decision-makers, combining thought leadership, the latest Oracle AI product updates, live demonstrations and one-to-one advisory conversations. The morning briefing moves from strategy to live innovation, followed by a networking lunch and optional private advisory slots in the afternoon. One Oracle. More value. Limitless innovation.

What you can expect

  • Executive insights into how AI, data and enterprise applications are reshaping the way organisations operate

  • The newest embedded AI capabilities across Oracle Fusion Applications

  • Release highlights and AI enhancements in Oracle APEX that accelerate enterprise innovation

  • Live demos of intelligent enterprise applications built on Oracle APEX and Oracle AI

  • A practical view of building an AI-ready data foundation with unified analytics

  • Private 1:1 executive advisory discussions with Oracle and Kovaion experts (afternoon, by request)

  • A networking lunch to exchange perspectives with peers and specialists

The day at a glance

  • 09:30 – 13:30 - Main session and networking lunch

  • 13:30 – 17:00 - Optional 1:1 executive advisory sessions

Who should attend

This briefing is designed for CIOs and CTOs, Heads of Applications, IT and Operations, Data and AI leaders, and Transformation heads - the people setting the enterprise technology, AI and digital strategy agenda.

Featured speakers include

  • Simon Hunt - Head of Product Management, Oracle APEX, Oracle UK

  • Mohana Priya Venkatachalam - Chief Projects Officer, Kovaion UK

  • Srinivas Nadella - Strategic Accounts Manager, Kovaion UK

With more Oracle and industry speakers to be announced.

You can view full agenda here - https:/www.kovaion.com/kovaion-connect-2026/

Venue: Oracle Corporation UK Ltd., One South Place, London EC2M 2RB - minutes from Moorgate, Liverpool Street and Bank stations, with rail, Underground and Elizabeth line connections.

Its headline Greater Manchester Index fell from 15 in the first quarter of the year to 13 in Q2 Firms polled said they had been affected by dips in domestic demand and advance orders, while the service sector had seen a decline and construction had seen two consecutive quarters of contraction. Service exports also fell, but manufacturing exports rose as that sector showed s The QES figures were revealed at the Greater Manchester Business Index event, held by the Chamber and the Growth Company at The Manchester College’s City Campus Subrahmaniam Krishnan-Harihara, director of business policy and research at the Chamber, said: “The key issue is that we’ve had a long period of low-level economic growth. input.summary: Manchester inward investment: ‘Reasons for optimism’ despite fall in FDI schemes City attracted 31 FDI projects in 2025 – down from 44 in 2024 Economic Development Alistair Houghton Editor, Business Live 05:00, 23 Jun 2026 Manchester City Centre se Manchester was Britain's best-p
input.evidence_text: Manchester inward investment: ‘Reasons for optimism’ despite fall in FDI schemes City attracted 31 FDI projects in 2025 – down from 44 in 2024 Economic Development Alistair Houghton Editor, Business Live 05:00, 23 Jun 2026 Manchester City Centre se Manchester was Britain's best-performing city outside London for attracting Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in 2025 despite a fall in the number of projects, new figures from EY have shown The latest EY 2026 UK Attractiveness Survey, which ranked 259 European regions on the number of FDI projects they attracted last year, showed Manchester attracted 31 FDI projects in 2025 – down from 44 in 2024. That made it the best-performing city outside of London for FDI for t Key FDI sectors included software and IT services, with six projects, with four projects in the health and social work sector and another four in finance, business and professional services The UK saw a total of 730 FDI projects last year, down 14% on 853 in 2024. The North West was the fourth ranked region in the UK in terms of project numbers with 51, behind Greater London (279 projects), Scotland (108 projects) and the West Midlands (68 projects) Of the UK’s nations and regions, only Greater London, Wales and Northern Ireland saw year-on-year growth in FDI projects, with the South West flat. All other regions reported declines, with the North West seeing a 41% year-on-year fall

input.evidence_text: Economic Development Andy Burnham wins Makerfield: When will the next Greater Manchester mayor be elected now he is an MP? Huge win in by-election paves way for Labour leadership challenge Economic Development Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 07:33, 19 Jun 2026 Andy Burnham Andy Burnham is heading back to Parliament after winning the Makerfield by-election The newly elected Labour MP came out on top in the race against a strong challenge from Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon But overnight Mr Burnham secured a seismic win, with 24,927 votes placing him well ahead of Reform challenger Robert Kenyon who secured 15,696 votes. His return to Parliament is expected to pave the way for a leadership challenge against Prime Minister Keir Starmer It marks the end of weeks of campaigning in the constituency based south of Wigan, and the start of the next political race in Greater Manchester for Andy Burnham's old job as mayor Becoming an MP disqualifies Burnham from being Greater Manchester mayor, so a replacement needs to be found for the region's top political job
